Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 [Read]: Search emails for BrightLoop apartment and vehicle issues using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "BrightLoop" or "apartment"). Find Simone Richter's complaint to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (email_id: email_email_b6ce20dc2587, subject: "Apartment issue — I was placed in a studio, not a 1BR"). Discover she has been in a studio for almost three weeks instead of the promised 1BR. Also search for Marcus Webb's vehicle delay — find Road Runner Auto Transport's email to blessing.okafor@moveops.com (email_id: email_email_a3ca1b6dd238, subject: "Delay update: Marcus Webb 2019 Honda Civic shipment to Boston"). Blessing missed this email.
OE 2 [Read]: Search emails for the UK vendor cold email using search_emails (query: "Meridian" or "UK relocation" or "partnership"). Find the original cold email from partnerships@meridianglobalmobility.co.uk to david.chen@moveops.com (email_id: email_email_fb6dd636cc46, subject: "Potential UK-US relocation partnership for MoveOps clients") from Asha Patel, Partnerships Director at Meridian Global Mobility. Also find David's forward to Mina (email_id: email_email_fa3003e43666). This is the only UK vendor lead for Oliver Bancroft's London transfer.
OE 3 [Read]: Search emails for Tessa Moreno's May expansion request using search_emails (query: "Tessa" or "May relocations" or "six"). Find email from tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (email_id: email_email_4ea76cf653a9, subject: "Six May Boston relocations + board visibility"). Discover six employees: Oliver Bancroft (London — UK transfer), Priya Naidu, Jonathan Marsh, Leah Kwon, Daniel Okafor, and Sofia Reyes.
OE 4 [Read]: Search Slack for BrightLoop internal discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "BrightLoop" or "Simone" or "Marcus Webb"). Find Julian Brooks's service recovery messages in #customer-support (C007) and #customer-engagement (C002) — Julian took over both the Simone apartment and Marcus Webb vehicle issues. Also find Chloe's capacity concerns and Mina's escalation about the combined BrightLoop crisis.
OE 5 [Read]: Search Airtable for BrightLoop relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "BrightLoop Analytics"). Find records for Simone Richter (recSimoneRichterBrightloop, In Progress, Suki Patel) and Marcus Webb (recMarcusWebbBrightloop, In Progress, Suki Patel). Check whether records reflect the apartment and vehicle issues or need updating.
OE 6 [Read]: Search Linear for BrightLoop-related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "BrightLoop"). Find linear_issue_c16357d188c6 ("BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion") and linear_issue_f85be674c9b8 ("Document BrightLoop ops gaps: Marcus vendor miss, Simone housing trace, Priya ADA handling, Oliver UK workflow"). These show existing tracking of the issues.
OE 7 [Read]: Search CRM for BrightLoop using crm_search_companies (name: "BrightLoop"). Then crm_search_deals to find deal_brightloop_apr2026 (April 2026 relocations, closedwon) and deal_brightloop_q2 (Q2 relocation, qualifiedtobuy). Check engagement notes for context on the expansion request and unresolved issues.
OE 8 [Read]: Search emails for StormCloud escalation using search_emails (query: "Liam Park" or "StormCloud" or "leadership review"). Find Liam Park's escalation email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (email_id: email_email_7b0cc9a6d376, subject: "Urgent before tomorrow's leadership review"). Discover: Jae-won Kim still in temp housing with no signed lease, promised $500 goodwill credit missing from invoice, Simone Richter's desk reimbursement stuck, and Anya Petrova's Ridgeline referral unanswered.
OE 9 [Read]: Search emails for Jae-won Kim lease issue using search_emails (query: "Jae-won" or "lease" or "guarantor"). Find UrbanNest's Carmen Reyes email about the unsigned lease (email_id: email_email_0dfe51bca72b, subject: "Urgent: Jae-won Kim Austin lease still pending guarantor packet") and Jae-won's own follow-up (email_id: email_email_1a3fb965161a). Suki was supposed to send guarantor paperwork but got buried in the Deshawn Hartley trade show scramble.
OE 10 [Read]: Search Slack for David's Ridgeline referral celebration using conversations_search_messages (query: "Ridgeline" or "referral" or "Anya"). Find David Chen's message in #customer-engagement (C002) celebrating Anya Petrova's warm intro to Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin batch of 5 relocations. Then search CRM for Ridgeline using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline") — find that a company record (crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2) and deal (crm_deal_a95b4721982a, "Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch") DO exist, but verify whether anyone actually contacted Kenji. Search emails for "Ridgeline" or "Kenji" to find Kenji's unanswered follow-up (email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1) and any belated responses from David/Mina after the escalation.
OE 11 [Read]: Search Airtable for Jae-won Kim's StormCloud record using airtable_search_records (field_name: "Name", value: "Jae-won Kim"). Find record recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin (StormCloud Analytics, In Progress, Suki Patel). The special requirements note states "Lease still unsigned — UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork. Record incorrectly shows Completed." The Status field currently shows "In Progress" — Chloe may have already corrected it, but the note references the prior incorrect status.
OE 12 [Read]: Search Linear for StormCloud-related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "StormCloud" or "Jae-won"). Find linear_issue_c97359216490 ("StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS") which was closed prematurely per linear_issue_a49b4d199139 ("StormCloud account incident reconstruction"). Also find linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 ("Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents") about the misprioritized support ticket.
OE 13 [Read]: Search emails or QuickBooks for the $500 goodwill credit issue using search_emails (query: "goodwill credit" or "EST-2026-0287" or "$500 credit"). Find Marcus Thorne's email (email_id: email_email_ed395722f1d4) discovering the $500 credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319. The credit issue is documented in email, not as a QuickBooks line item.
OE 14 [Read]: Look up contact details using contacts_search_contacts for David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) and Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com).
OE 15 [Write]: Update Airtable records for BrightLoop relocations using airtable_update_records if any details are wrong or missing — add notes about the apartment mismatch for Simone Richter (recSimoneRichterBrightloop) and the vehicle shipment delay for Marcus Webb (recMarcusWebbBrightloop).
OE 16 [Write]: Update Airtable record for Jae-won Kim (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) using airtable_update_records if the status or notes need correction — confirm the unsigned lease situation is reflected and the record accurately represents the current state.
OE 17 [Write]: Create Linear issues or add comments to existing tickets using linear_create_issue (team: "team_operations") or linear_create_comment for any unresolved items not already tracked. BrightLoop items: Simone apartment resolution, Marcus Webb vehicle recovery, UK vendor outreach for Oliver Bancroft. StormCloud items: Jae-won lease guarantor packet, Ridgeline referral follow-up, $500 credit correction. Check existing tickets (linear_issue_c16357d188c6, linear_issue_f85be674c9b8, linear_issue_a49b4d199139) before creating duplicates.
OE 18 [Write]: Send email to David Chen via send_email (sender: chloe.vance@moveops.com, recipients: [david.chen@moveops.com]). The email should cover the client risk picture across both accounts: BrightLoop's broken April batch (Simone apartment, Marcus Webb car), readiness gaps for the May expansion (no UK vendor, no white-glove pricing), and StormCloud's unresolved failures (unsigned lease, dropped Ridgeline referral, misapplied $500 credit). Include what's been fixed and what David needs to act on before Friday.
OE 19 [Write]: Post a summary in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006). Cover: BrightLoop unresolved items from April (Simone apartment, Marcus Webb vehicle), StormCloud open issues (Jae-won lease, Ridgeline referral), what records were corrected, and what tickets were created or updated.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_list_tables) to look up relocation records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_list_tables and airtable_search_records multiple times (searching for BrightLoop and StormCloud records in the Relocations table, and Stipend Transactions). These are legitimate Airtable read tool calls.
✓Run #2The agent used `airtable_search_records` multiple times (for BrightLoop and StormCloud relocation records), and also `airtable_list_tables` and `airtable_list_bases` to discover and query relocation data.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_list_tables, airtable_search_records (multiple times for BrightLoop and StormCloud clients), and airtable_list_records to look up relocation records. These tool calls are clearly present in the trajectory.
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times (searching BrightLoop and StormCloud relocations, stipend transactions) and airtable_list_tables. These are Airtable read tools confirming relocation records were looked up.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables (twice), and airtable_search_records (for both BrightLoop and StormCloud companies, plus Stipend Transactions) to look up relocation records.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables (appMoveOpsOps001), and airtable_search_records multiple times to look up BrightLoop and StormCloud relocation records in the Relocations table.
2 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues, linear_get_issue, or linear_list_comments) to search for existing tickets.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times querying for 'BrightLoop', 'StormCloud', 'Jae-won', 'Simone Richter', 'Marcus Webb', 'Ridgeline', 'Road Runner', 'Oliver Bancroft', and 'Simone apartment housing'. These are clear Linear read tool calls to search for existing tickets.
✓Run #2The agent used `linear_list_issues` multiple times (querying 'BrightLoop', 'StormCloud', 'Jae-won', 'Simone Richter', 'Marcus Webb', 'Ridgeline', 'Oliver Bancroft', 'credit goodwill'), and also `linear_get_issue` for specific issue IDs.
✓Run #3The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries for 'BrightLoop', 'StormCloud', 'Jae-won', 'Simone Richter', 'Marcus Webb', 'Ridgeline', and 'Oliver Bancroft' to search for existing tickets before creating new ones.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries including 'BrightLoop', 'StormCloud', 'Jae-won', 'Simone Richter', 'Marcus Webb', 'Oliver Bancroft', 'Road Runner', and 'UrbanNest' to search for existing tickets before creating new ones.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries for 'BrightLoop', 'StormCloud', 'Jae-won', 'Simone Richter', 'Marcus Webb', 'Ridgeline', 'Road Runner', 'Oliver Bancroft', and 'stipend', confirming it searched for existing tickets.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries including 'BrightLoop', 'StormCloud', 'Simone Richter', 'Marcus Webb', 'Jae-won', 'Ridgeline', and 'stipend Simone' to search for existing tickets.
3 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_companies, crm_search_deals, or crm_list_engagements) to look up account or deal information.
tool selection
